title: Removed "x+", "x-", "y+", and "y-" from footprintInsertionDirection
description: Removes the directional options x, x-, y, and y- from the footprint insertion direction type definition, streamlining the available options for users.

title: Refactor CAD offset logic to use model bounds + SVG origin extraction
description: This pull request refactors the CAD offset logic to utilize model bounds and extract the SVG origin. It introduces a new method for calculating the CAD model offset based on the bounds of the model, improving the accuracy of the placement of CAD models in the circuit design. The changes include updates to the conversion functions and adjustments to the handling of CAD model properties, ensuring that the models origin is correctly calculated and applied during the conversion process.

body: https://3d-viewer-git-fork-abse2001-main-tscircuit.vercel.app/?path=/story/keypad--default before after

title: Infer internal footprint connections for split pins and shared aliases
description: This change makes repeated non-overlapping footprint contacts behave like implicit internally connected pins instead of ambiguous PCB targets, allowing traces to target shared aliases directly and updating port matching accordingly.
body:
This makes repeated non-overlapping footprint contacts behave like implicit internally connected pins instead of ambiguous PCB targets. Separate physical contact clusters now become separate source ports with one PCB port each, and they are tied together through existing internal-connection machinery with no schema change. Shared non-numeric aliases like A1 also infer the same internal connection, so traces can target the alias directly.

  It also updates pad and plated-hole matching to assign each primitive to the correct inferred port, removes the old ambiguous-port expectations from imported footprint tests, and adds repro coverage with PCB SVG snapshots for split-pin and battery clip cases.

title: chore: update packages @tscircuit/checks
description: Updates the tscircuitchecks package from version 0.0.119 to 0.0.120 in the package.json file.

title: Update props/circuit-json versions and migrate board spacing fields to clearance
description: Updates DRC properties in the board schema from spacing to clearance to align with upstream package changes and resolves TypeScript errors.
body:
### Motivation
- Upstream packages renamed DRC properties from `spacing` to `clearance`, causing TypeScript errors and mismatches between parsed props and `circuit-json` types. 
- The board DRC schema now exposes `min_board_edge_clearance` in the updated JLC tolerances and it should be propagated into inserted `PcbBoard` objects.

### Description
- Bumped dependencies in `package.json`: `@tscircuit/props` → `^0.0.510`, `circuit-json` → `^0.0.418`, and updated `@tscircuit/jlcpcb-manufacturing-specs` to commit `5644e294acfdd545ceb468c3b95c9ed82cb9a00b`.
- Replaced board field mappings in `lib/components/normal-components/Board.ts` from `min_trace_to_pad_spacing`/`min_pad_to_pad_spacing` to `min_trace_to_pad_clearance`/`min_pad_to_pad_clearance` and added `min_board_edge_clearance` sourced from subcircuit props or `jlcMinTolerances`.
- Updated the test `tests/components/normal-components/board-manufacture-drc-property.test.tsx` to use the new prop names `minTraceToPadClearance`/`minPadToPadClearance` and assert the corresponding PCB board fields.

### Testing
- Ran typecheck with `bunx tsc --noEmit` which completed successfully.
- Ran the updated test with `bun test tests/components/normal-components/board-manufacture-drc-property.test.tsx` and it passed (1 test, 0 failures).

------
[Codex Task](https://chatgpt.com/codex/cloud/tasks/task_b_69e7bd4745d083279464709e3e8f0652)

title: update footprinter dep
description: Updates the version of the tscircuitfootprinter dependency from 0.0.349 to 0.0.351 in package.json

title: Use jlcMinTolerances values for DRC defaults
description: Aligns local DRC default values with the published JLCPCB minimum manufacturing tolerances, replacing hardcoded values with dynamic values from the jlcMinTolerances package.
body:
### Motivation
- Align local DRC default values with the published JLCPCB minimum manufacturing tolerances so checks use the manufacturer minimums rather than hardcoded values.

### Description
- Add `@tscircuit/jlcpcb-manufacturing-specs` to `devDependencies` pinned to commit `fa54ff1f9eabca1b852a0b035db907061e5c4a2f` in `package.json`.
- Import `jlcMinTolerances` from `@tscircuit/jlcpcb-manufacturing-specs` in `lib/drc-defaults.ts` and use its fields as the source for DRC minimums.
- Replace hardcoded defaults with expressions that prefer `jlcMinTolerances` values and fall back to the previous numeric constants where the package does not supply a value.
- Preserve unrelated constants like `DEFAULT_VIA_BOARD_MARGIN` and `EPSILON` unchanged.

### Testing
- Ran `bun run format`, which completed but reported existing large-file warnings for JSON fixtures; formatting run finished (warnings only).
- Performed `npm install` (git dependency resolved) and `npm run build`, and the build completed successfully.

------

title: fix: preserve source pin identity in KiCad pad numbers (#212)
description: Fixes KiCad pad labeling to preserve source pin identity instead of array order, ensuring correct pad numbering for custom footprints.
body:
## Summary

- KiCad export was labelling pads by `pcb_smtpad` / `pcb_plated_hole` array order instead of by source pin identity, so a custom footprint declared in reverse physical order (`portHints={["pin4"]}` at x=-3, ..., `portHints={["pin1"]}` at x=3) came out with inverted KiCad pad labels.
- Added `getKiCadPadNumber` — walks `pcb_port → source_port.pin_number`, with fallbacks to pin-like `port_hints` (`"pin4"` → `"4"`, BGA-style `"A1"` → `"A1"`) and the existing sequential counter as last resort.
- Threaded the resolver through `convertSmdPads` / `convertPlatedHoles` as an optional `getPadNumber` callback, mirroring the existing `getNetInfo` pattern in `AddFootprintsStage`.

Fixes #212.

## Before vs after

Same four-pad footprint from the issue repro (`portHints` in reverse order):

| pad position `(at x y)` | before | after |
|---|---|---|
| `-3 0` | `pad "1"` | `pad "4"` |
| `-1 0` | `pad "2"` | `pad "3"` |
| ` 1 0` | `pad "3"` | `pad "2"` |
| ` 3 0` | `pad "4"` | `pad "1"` |

## Notes

- The pad UUID seed includes `padNumber` (`pad:${componentId}:${padNumber}:${x},${y}`), so UUIDs for affected pads will change once. This is desirable — UUIDs now track pin identity rather than array order, so reordering pads in source no longer churns them.
- `getPadNumber` is optional on both converter functions to keep the signatures backward-compatible for any external callers.
- Fallback counter keeps incrementing every iteration so unresolved pads on the sequence-number fallback path don't collide with each other.

## Test plan

- [x] New regression test `tests/pcb/repros/repro13-custom-footprint-pad-numbering.test.tsx` — exact repro from the issue, asserts pad `"4"` is at x=-3, pad `"1"` at x=3, etc.
- [x] `bun run typecheck` clean
- [x] `bun run format:check` clean
- [ ] CI green (including PNG snapshot tests that need `kicad-cli`)

title: Polish mobile feature cards and footer layout
description: Enhances the small-screen experience on the landing page by tightening the feature card layout, simplifying repeated card styles, and redesigning the footer for mobile to read as a compact sitemap instead of a squeezed desktop stack.
body:
Improve the small-screen experience on the landing page by tightening the feature card layout, simplifying repeated card styles, and redesigning the footer for mobile so it reads as a compact, intentional
  sitemap instead of a squeezed desktop stack.

  Changes:

  - Simplify feature card CSS and remove repeated inline styling
  - Center and constrain feature cards on very small screens
  - Convert the mobile footer into compact link-group cards
  - Improve spacing and hierarchy in the footer brand and note area

  Validation:

  - Visual/layout change only
  - No automated tests added

/closes https://github.com/tscircuit/tscircuit.com/issues/3220

title: Replace autorouting_example.mp4 with autorouting_animation.mov; update video source and styles for better presentation
description: Updates the landing page with improved UI elements, navigation simplification, animated GitHub star count, and replaces the autorouting demo video.
body:
This pull request updates the `index.html` file with several improvements to the UI, navigation, and user experience, focusing on enhanced feature descriptions, improved GitHub star count display, and minor visual tweaks. The most important changes are grouped below.

### UI and Feature Description Improvements

* Updated feature descriptions for AI coding skills, open source/forkability, and analog simulation to include clearer language and relevant links [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-0eb547304658805aad788d320f10bf1f292797b5e6d745a3bf617584da017051L1463-R1475) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-0eb547304658805aad788d320f10bf1f292797b5e6d745a3bf617584da017051L1502-R1507) [[3]](diffhunk://#diff-0eb547304658805aad788d320f10bf1f292797b5e6d745a3bf617584da017051L1513-R1522).
* Added inline background colors to preview images for PCB and schematic panels for better visual clarity.
* Added a "Quick Start" button linking to the CLI quickstart guide for easier onboarding.

### Navigation Simplification

* Removed the redundant "Product" link from both the desktop and mobile navigation menus to streamline navigation [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-0eb547304658805aad788d320f10bf1f292797b5e6d745a3bf617584da017051L1279) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-0eb547304658805aad788d320f10bf1f292797b5e6d745a3bf617584da017051L1302).

### GitHub Star Count Animation and Fallback

* Implemented animated counting for the GitHub star count, including improved formatting and a fallback value for better user engagement and resilience to API failures [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-0eb547304658805aad788d320f10bf1f292797b5e6d745a3bf617584da017051R1844-R1847) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-0eb547304658805aad788d320f10bf1f292797b5e6d745a3bf617584da017051L1962-R2033) [[3]](diffhunk://#diff-0eb547304658805aad788d320f10bf1f292797b5e6d745a3bf617584da017051L1987-R2062) [[4]](diffhunk://#diff-0eb547304658805aad788d320f10bf1f292797b5e6d745a3bf617584da017051R2322-R2325).

### Minor Visual and Media Updates

* Changed the `object-fit` CSS property from `cover` to `fit` for media elements, and updated the autorouting demo video and its container styling [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-0eb547304658805aad788d320f10bf1f292797b5e6d745a3bf617584da017051L375-R375) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-0eb547304658805aad788d320f10bf1f292797b5e6d745a3bf617584da017051L1463-R1475).

These changes collectively improve the clarity, interactivity, and visual polish of the landing page.

title: Transform pcb_component insertion metadata in transformPCBElements
description: Updates transformPCBElement to maintain consistent pcb_component geometric metadata by transforming insertion_direction and moving cable_insertion_center with the applied matrix, while ensuring correct handling of quarter-turns for negative rotations.
body: Update transformPCBElement to keep pcb_component geometric metadata consistent by transforming insertion_direction from matrix rotation plus PCB-space Y flip, and by moving cable_insertion_center with the applied matrix. This also switches quarter-turn handling to use absolute parity so width/height swaps stay correct for negative rotations as well.

title: Rename SubcircuitGroupProps manufacturing DRC fields to updated camelCase names
description: Renames DRC-related props on SubcircuitGroupProps to consistent camelCase names for runtime validation and documentation.
body:
### Motivation
- The manufacturing DRC-related prop names on `SubcircuitGroupProps` were updated and needed to be reflected in the codebase using consistent camelCase naming.
- Runtime validation and generated documentation must match the new property names to avoid consumer/CI breakage.

### Description
- Renamed the DRC-related props on the `SubcircuitGroupProps` TypeScript interface in `lib/components/group.ts` to `minViaHoleEdgeToViaHoleEdgeClearance`, `minPlatedHoleDrillEdgeToDrillEdgeClearance`, `minTraceToPadEdgeClearance`, and `minPadEdgeToPadEdgeClearance` while keeping other existing fields unchanged.
- Updated the `subcircuitGroupProps` Zod schema in `lib/components/group.ts` to use the same renamed keys for runtime validation.
- Regenerated the derived documentation files so generated artifacts reflect the change, updating `generated/COMPONENT_TYPES.md` and `generated/PROPS_OVERVIEW.md` accordingly.

### Testing
- Ran the required generation scripts: `bun scripts/generate-component-types.ts`, `bun scripts/generate-manual-edits-docs.ts`, `bun scripts/generate-readme-docs.ts`, and `bun scripts/generate-props-overview.ts`, which completed successfully.
- Ran the focused test suite `bun test tests/group.test.ts`, which passed (16 tests, 0 failures).

------

title: Add rectBorderRadius support to pill_hole_with_rect_pad plated holes
description: Adds support for rectBorderRadius in pill_hole_with_rect_pad plated holes, allowing for customizable corner rounding in PCB design.

title: Wire footprint insertion direction into PCB components and align board DRC fields
description: Propagates footprint.insertionDirection onto pcb_component.insertion_direction using the components global pre-layout rotation plus bottom-side mirroring, ensuring correct behavior for bottom-authored footprints and footprint constraints, while aligning board manufacturing defaults and tests with new clearance field names in circuit-json.
body:
Propagate footprint.insertionDirection onto pcb_component.insertion_direction using the component’s global pre-layout rotation plus bottom-side mirroring, and make primitive footprint flipping respect originalLayer so bottom-authored footprints and footprint constraints behave correctly. Bump @tscircuit/circuit-json-util to rely on its built-in PCB component transform updates.

  Also align board manufacturing defaults and tests with the new *Clearance field names in circuit-json, replace the stale JLC tolerance import with a local helper, and add regression coverage for transformed insertion directions and originalLayer="bottom" footprint layout.

title: Propagate board min via dimensions through autorouting
description: Propagates board minimum via dimensions through autorouting by updating the autorouter to use specific via dimensions from the board, ensuring routed via dimensions are preserved and not overwritten by defaults.
body:
Bump @tscircuit/capacity-autorouter to pick up min via pad/hole support, extend core’s local SRJ types to carry the new fields, and pass board min_via_pad_diameter / min_via_hole_diameter into the autorouter input.

  Also preserve routed via dimensions when materializing pcb_via records so autorouter-provided values are not overwritten by generic defaults.

Also update the default simulation palette to more standard sim colors and add coverage for the full-bridge differential probe case.

title: Fix package details dialog stacking in Import Component flow
description: Fixes the Import Component package details view so clicking See Details no longer appears broken when the details dialog opens behind the search dialog.
body:
Before: 


https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/c62607db-251a-49fc-8e4e-20bc59c0fcf5


After:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/e6f8c768-94f4-4acb-acbc-94b7799f0678

Fixes the Import Component package details view so clicking “See Details” no longer appears broken when the details dialog opens behind the search dialog.

The issue was caused by the search dialog using a higher z-index than the package details dialog, which left the details dialog mounted but hidden in the visual stack. This change raises the details dialog z-index above the search dialog.

title: Honor explicit via pad/hole dimensions across autorouters

body:
Normalize SRJ via dimensions through a shared helper so all autorouting pipelines and solvers use min_via_pad_diameter ahead of deprecated minViaDiameter. Pipeline4 now also propagates the requested hole diameter into simplified routes.

  Update circuit-json export and SVG rendering to preserve and draw the routed via size correctly, including via_hole_diameter on emitted vias.

title: Fix component-owned silkscreen paths exporting as board graphics
description: Fixes KiCad export for pcb_silkscreen_path objects that belong to a component, ensuring they are exported as footprint-local primitives instead of board-level graphics.
body:
Before: 

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/e8837da8-4d2f-4fad-882d-dc4039057b63

After: 

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/ab8d963a-161e-401e-aca1-ad980414b8d0

Fixes KiCad export for pcb_silkscreen_path objects that belong to a component.

Before this change, component-owned silkscreen paths were exported as board-level gr_line graphics instead of footprint-local primitives. That made the board look mostly correct at first, but the silkscreen became detached from the part in KiCad. If a user moved or flipped the component, the silkscreen outline stayed behind instead of moving with it.

This change keeps standalone silkscreen paths exported as board graphics, but exports component-owned silkscreen paths inside the footprint as fp_line. The new conversion keeps the lines in footprint-local space, applies the component position and rotation correctly, and maps them to the proper silkscreen layer.

A repro test was updated to verify the behavior by checking that component-owned silkscreen paths now appear on the footprint and no longer leak into board-level silkscreen graphics.

title: Refresh landing page copy and self-host board gallery images
description: Updates the landing page copy for clarity, revises statistics, and optimizes gallery image loading by switching from remote URLs to local assets.
body:
This pull request updates the `index.html` landing page to improve clarity, update statistics, and optimize gallery image loading. The most important changes are summarized below.

**Content and Messaging Updates:**

* The hero section's subheading was shortened by removing the reference to shipping to JLCPCB, making the message more concise.
* The description for KiCad support was rewritten to clarify that both import and export are supported, and to better explain native KiCad PCM integration.

**Statistical and Terminology Updates:**

* The "Public repos" stat was updated from 289 to 304, and "GH followers" was replaced with "Contributors", updating the count from 209 to 397, reflecting more relevant and current metrics.

**Gallery Image Optimization:**

* Gallery board images were changed from remote API URLs to local asset files, which should improve page load times and reliability. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-0eb547304658805aad788d320f10bf1f292797b5e6d745a3bf617584da017051L1757-R1756) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-0eb547304658805aad788d320f10bf1f292797b5e6d745a3bf617584da017051L1766-R1773)

title: Rename JLCPCB tolerance keys, adjust defaults, and bump circuit-json dependency
description: Renames JLCPCB tolerance keys for clarity, adjusts default tolerance values for accuracy, and updates the circuit-json dependency to ensure compatibility with the latest board schema.
body:
### Motivation
- Make the JLCPCB tolerance keys explicit about edge-to-edge measurements and align names with updated PCB board schema.
- Adjust a couple of default tolerance values to more accurate minima for plated holes and preferred hole sizes.
- Ensure compatibility with the latest board schema by bumping the `circuit-json` dependency.

### Description
- Renamed tolerance keys in `lib/jlcpcb-manufacturing-specs.ts` from generic names like `min_via_to_via_clearance` to explicit names such as `min_via_hole_edge_to_via_hole_edge_clearance`, `min_plated_hole_drill_edge_to_drill_edge_clearance`, `min_trace_to_pad_edge_clearance`, and `min_pad_edge_to_pad_edge_clearance`.
- Increased `min_plated_hole_drill_edge_to_drill_edge_clearance` in `jlcMinTolerances` to `0.15` and updated preferred hole diameters to `min_via_hole_diameter: 0.4` and `min_via_pad_diameter: 0.6` in `jlcPreferredTolerances`.
- Bumped the `circuit-json` devDependency in `package.json` from `^0.0.418` to `^0.0.419`.

### Testing
- Ran type checking with `npm run typecheck` (which runs `tsc --noEmit`) and it completed successfully.
- Ran format/lint checks with `npm run format:check` and they passed without errors.
- No other automated test suites were added or modified as part of this change.

------
[Codex Task](https://chatgpt.com/codex/cloud/tasks/task_b_69ea586c9b008327832a28b7636fc11a)

title: Update dependencies and migrate PCB manufacturing DRC field names
description: Updates dependencies to the latest versions and migrates PCB manufacturing DRC field names to match the new schema in the circuit JSON.
body:
### Motivation

- Bump `circuit-json`/`@tscircuit/props` and pin `@tscircuit/jlcpcb-manufacturing-specs` to the specified commit so the project uses the latest PCB schema and prop names.
- Migrate board manufacturing DRC fields to the renamed keys in the latest `PcbBoard` schema so emitted circuit JSON matches the updated types.

### Description

- Updated `package.json` to use `circuit-json@^0.0.419`, `@tscircuit/props@^0.0.513`, and `@tscircuit/jlcpcb-manufacturing-specs` at commit `e08af159db01a37db007e33f0a7268d0e4a279a5`.
- Changed `lib/components/normal-components/Board.ts` to emit the new manufacturing DRC fields (`min_via_hole_edge_to_via_hole_edge_clearance`, `min_trace_to_pad_edge_clearance`, `min_pad_edge_to_pad_edge_clearance`, and `min_plated_hole_drill_edge_to_drill_edge_clearance`) mapping from the component props and JLC defaults.
- Updated tests and inline snapshot expectations in `tests/components/normal-components/board-manufacture-drc-property.test.tsx` and `tests/footprint/footprint-library-map3.test.tsx` to use the new prop names and PCB JSON keys.

### Testing

- Ran TypeScript check with `bunx tsc --noEmit` which succeeded.
- Ran the relevant tests with `bun test tests/components/normal-components/board-manufacture-drc-property.test.tsx tests/footprint/footprint-library-map3.test.tsx` and both tests passed (1 snapshot updated to match new schema).

------
[Codex Task](https://chatgpt.com/codex/cloud/tasks/task_b_69eb98e086048327b6c0b823150eb05b)

title: Improve Benchmark PR Feedback with Early Results, Main-Branch Deltas, and Resilient Profile Solver Timeouts
description: Enhances benchmark feedback by incorporating early results, comparing with main branch deltas, and implementing timeouts for profile solvers.

title: Add benchmark CI profile-solvers comparison
description: Adds --profile-solvers support to benchmark CI, enabling profile comparison tables for PR and main in benchmark results.
body:
Adds `--profile-solvers` support to benchmark CI. When included in a `/benchmark` comment or workflow dispatch, the workflow runs the existing `scripts/profile-solvers.ts` for both PR and main, uploads the profile artifacts, and renders main/PR profile comparison tables in the benchmark result comment.

Also extends `profile-solvers.ts` to support benchmark datasets/effort and emit `profile-solvers.json` for structured CI rendering.

title: Simplify BOM table rendering
description: Rewrites BomTable to render directly from structured BOM rows returned by circuit-json-to-bom-csv instead of deriving the table from source_component elements.
body: This PR rewrites BomTable to render directly from the structured BOM rows returned by circuit-json-to-bom-csv instead of deriving the table from source_component elements. What changed: - switched BOM table data flow to convertCircuitJsonToBomRows - removed the old supplier-column derivation from source_component - added lightweight metadata helpers for optional columns - kept JLCPCB part-number linking - preserved support for manufacturer/MPN pairs and extra BOM columns - aligned the table header and body through a shared descriptor source of truth Verification: - bunx biome check lib/components/BomTable.tsx - bunx tsc -p tsconfig.json --noEmit ## Before ## After

title: Skip zero-length trace segments from duplicate route points
description: Prevents zero-length trace segments from being emitted when autorouter route data contains consecutive duplicate points.
body:
This PR prevents zero-length trace segments from being emitted when autorouter route data contains consecutive duplicate points.

Previously, convertHdRouteToSimplifiedRoute preserved adjacent same-layer duplicates such as A -> A -> B, which could later become a degenerate A -> A copper segment in exported PCB output. That kind of ghost segment can trigger downstream DRC issues even when the visible routing looks correct.

title: Add ccwRotation to the pill shaped smtpads in the output of simpleRouteJson's
description: Adds counter-clockwise rotation support for pill-shaped SMT pads in the output of simpleRouteJson, enhancing the representation of these components in the routing process.

title: Add the checkTracesAreContiguous drc checks to the runAllRoutingChecks
description: This pull request adds the checkTracesAreContiguous design rule check (DRC) to the runAllRoutingChecks function. It enhances the PCB trace validation process by ensuring that traces are contiguous and properly aligned, which is crucial for maintaining signal integrity in circuit designs. The changes include modifications to the check-traces-are-contiguous logic and the integration of this check into the overall routing checks.

title: Fix hover labels rendering above shapes
description: Raise rect and polygon hover label boxes into a shared overlay layer so they render above overlapping graphics and apply the same z-index to the hovered rect container to avoid stacking-context issues.
body:
## Summary
- Raise rect and polygon hover label boxes into a shared overlay layer so they render above overlapping graphics.
- Apply the same z-index to the hovered rect container to avoid stacking-context issues.
- Add a shared tooltip layer constant for the interactive graphics components.

## Testing
- Unit tests passed across the graphics suite.
- Local build completed successfully.
- Interactive formatting checks passed.

title: fix(autorouter): resolve bugreport50 node cmn_645__sub_0_0
description: Fixes autorouting failure for node cmn_645__sub_0_0 by adjusting the port points threshold from 10 to 12.
body: This fixes `bugreport50` node `cmn_645__sub_0_0` by expanding `SingleLayerNoDifferentRootIntersectionsIntraNodeSolver.isApplicable` from `>10` to `>12` port points.

title: chore: bump version to v0.0.455
description: Automated package update

title: Remove vias being created inside obstacles
description: Summary Add a regression test and SVG snapshot for bugreport52-a9573e. Prevent vias from being generated inside same-net multilayer obstacles by routing those transitions with a dedicated through-obstacle solver. Thread obstacle and layer context through the high-density pipeline so the specialized solver can be selected.  Testing Added a unit regression test that checks cmn_184 uses the through-obstacle solver and that source_trace_3 has no via inside the pad. Verified the new SVG snapshot against the updated route visualization.
body:
## Summary
- Add a regression test and SVG snapshot for `bugreport52-a9573e`.
- Prevent vias from being generated inside same-net multilayer obstacles by routing those transitions with a dedicated through-obstacle solver.
- Thread obstacle and layer context through the high-density pipeline so the specialized solver can be selected.

## Testing
- Added a unit regression test that checks `cmn_184` uses the through-obstacle solver and that `source_trace_3` has no via inside the pad.
- Verified the new SVG snapshot against the updated route visualization.

title: Adds a full-board STM32F746G-DISCO circuit JSON fixture and regression coverage for the current copper pour boolean failure on the GND net.
description: The new repro captures that copper pour generation currently throws Cannot complete boolean operation for both: top-layer GND pour bottom-layer GND pour Both assertions use inline error snapshots so the test suite stays green while preserving a focused reproduction for the solver bug.
body: The new repro captures that copper pour generation currently throws `Cannot complete boolean operation` for both: - top-layer GND pour - bottom-layer GND pour Both assertions use inline error snapshots so the test suite stays green while preserving a focused reproduction for the solver bug.

title: Update install path and project name for GitHub source installs
description: Rename the package and visible project title to pcb-poly-hyper-graph, switch the package entrypoint to source-only libindex.ts for GitHub codeload installs, remove the tsup build path and npm publish workflow, and update the README with Bun codeload install instructions and source import examples.
body:
## Summary
- Rename the package and visible project title to `pcb-poly-hyper-graph`
- Switch the package entrypoint to source-only `lib/index.ts` for GitHub codeload installs
- Remove the `tsup` build path and npm publish workflow
- Update the README with Bun codeload install instructions and source import examples

## Testing
- `bunx tsc --noEmit`
- `bun test`
- `bun run format:check`
- Verified the package tarball contents stay source-only under `lib`
- Verified a fresh Bun consumer can install the tarball and import `pcb-poly-hyper-graph`
